data.title: ASML Results Update: Price Power Remains Key In A Growing Market
data.description:
At current market valuation levels, the earnings season will add crucial information for the continuation of the recent bull market. ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) is traditionally among the first firms to report, together with some banks, and may set the tone for the expected continuation of positive results related to companies operating in the AI environment. This happens in an environment where rich valuations and historical highs are pushed by general margin benefits in several sectors in the US market, potentially pushed by productivity gains related to AI. Nevertheless, both in terms of margins and valuations, the AI hype has the potential to scare investors, even considering the solid sales and margins increases, reflecting benefits on cash conversion for a consistent number of players. A one-year P/E evaluation with the most important players in the AI hardware landscape reveals some sort of stability, and in some cases moderation, in the ratio, despite the stock performance as a result of consistent earnings growth. The positiveness around ASML in the premarket on the Oct/15th is, in this article, to be analyzed as a trade reaction or a fundamental based movement.

Investment Thesis

In a previous article, I mentioned the scientific basis of Intel. ASML is another firm that can trace its history to this edge-of-knowledge development from its foundation to current days. Founded in 1984 in the Netherlands, ASML succeeded in a competitive market, facing both American and Japanese contenders, to finally establish itself as a sort of monopoly in the top-tier lithography market. The equipment, initially conceived by Jay Lathrop which decided to invert the lenses of his microscope to illuminate photo-sensible chemicals on a chipset and therefore print integrated circuits, played a critical role in allowing for continuous size reduction and circuits multiplication in one chipset.

Nowadays, the market can be divided in two main products. The Deep Ultraviolet (DUV) products are standard equipment for chip production. They allow for mass production of chipsets that are not in the forefront of capacity, as the technology and the light wavelength used produce a larger size circuit. At this point, our society is moving to a chip-everywhere situation, meaning that the production and demand for general purpose chipset remains strong, pushing for expansion of production and lithography.

The most developed market, on the other hand, utilizes Ultra-Deep Ultraviolet (EUV), which is capable to print integrated circuits that are moving towards 3nm (nanometer). Yes! One nanometer is equal to one billionth of a meter – for the sake of comparison, a human hair has 80K – 100K nm wide. EVU’s expected CAGR is 11.8% to 2034. As one can imagine, such a small circuit allow for the agglomeration of almost countless number of transistors into one chipset, opening the field for computing capacity increases that is unthinkable using previous technologies. This is the exact point where the growing computing demand of AI is only achievable as these advanced machines are utilized in chip production.

If the demand for AI models and applications is growing and expected to remain elevated in the foreseeable future, the same applies to the hardware powering these solutions, more specifically the GPUs. As these very high capacity and efficiency GPUs are only viable through the utilization of advanced lithography equipment, it is possible to infer that the demand is healthy. Moreover, the fact that ASML reigns in this environment, it is also possible to infer that the company possesses a relatively strong pricing power in these products, even despite a certain concentration in the customers. All these conclusions are fair, but any evaluation of the lithography thesis can only be done after some analysis on the geopolitical aspect of the semiconductor market.

The many sovereign AI efforts around the globe produce a duplication effect on the investment. The company identified around US$ 200 million in direct government support in terms of tax credits and direct investment in the US, Europe, India, China, South Korea, Taiwan and Japan. This effect may be magnified if considered that supply chain internalization is under process in many nations, resulting in 5 – 8% extra foundry capacity on top of ‘normal’ expansion driven by demand.

In the first half of 2024, China represented 41.6% of total turnover. During this year, an escalation in trade tensions, especially in the tech sector, took place. The updated version of Advanced Computing and Semiconductor Manufacturing Rule resulted in export restrictions on EUV and certain DUV equipment. In October, further US export controls on chips impose additional impositions for the lithography supplier. From a Chinese perspective, retaliations related to rare earths and minerals have the potential to disrupt ASML’s supply chain. Moreover, Pekin also recently introduced import restrictions on semiconductors and related equipment, trying to foster domestic development and independence. As a result, the participation of China in the top line reduced to 24%, with both Taiwan and the US increasing purchases from ASML in the first half of the year. In Q3, nevertheless, deliveries to China increased back to 42%. The uncertainty around the China trade brings two important implications to ASML. First, quarterly results may present abnormal volatility, depending on unpredictable escalation and de-escalation. As a result, a financial burden is imposed to the firm. Secondly, despite still not relevant, China may want to develop own solutions and foster a future competitor to ASML, more or less in the same pattern as happening with Huawei and Nvidia (NVDA). Right now, SMEE (Shanghai Micro Electronics Equipment) has no EUV capabilities and is not in the position to compete directly with ASML top tier equipment. Moreover, investors may understand this segment as a high-entrance cost market, where massive investment is needed to develop new products, subjected to a variable success rate. As this is a future risk, ASML has strong fundamentals to remain tech dominant with strategic tailwinds in the medium term, at least.

Fundamental Score

In my research, I compare several target firms that are selected based on an appropriate investment thesis. Right now, some of the relevant theses include Artificial Intelligence Hardware and Applications, Infrastructure and Cybersecurity, and Defense among others. Firms are compared in terms of four elements. Initially, the Cash Discipline is evaluated in terms of how well the operation converts cash. The second is related to the total yield an investor receives considering the payout and the potential reinvestment of cash at current prices. An analysis of growth components is the next category, in order to understand commercial success and how it translates into real profitability growth. The last one is a measure of momentum, as it is a strong performance indicator and also may impose an important demand engine due to the prevalence of passive investments.

In general, ASML is rated as a Hold, with strong performance on cash and momentum grades. This indicates that, in the current price, there is a mix of strong fundamentals but also the momentum trade related to AI in general. On Figure 1, an evaluation of last 4 years of cash flow management is performed. The numbers are scaled by last year sales to allow for comparison with other firms. ASML generates consistent operating cash flow, is such levels that is sufficient to absorb required investments and return capital to shareholders. In general, an increase in the debt level, as occurred in Q2/2023, is directly related to the cash variation.

The positive management of cash, reflected in the Cash Grade, can also be perceived in the balance sheet indicators. Since the acquisition of Hermes Microvision in 2016, ASML is not actively engaged in large M&A transactions. The goodwill recorded remained relatively flat, which implied in steady reduction of the ratio to other long-term assets. In this way, one can understand the firm’s numbers as organically generated. The debt level is also being reduced since 2022, as a result of an increase in shareholders equity from Retained Earnings (i.e. from operating results). In terms of liquidity, current ratio reduced to 1.3 in the period. It is hard to make a shorter-term assessment due to report limitations on the Current Liabilities, but a long-term analysis depicts an increase in unearned revenue, linked to production slot prepayments for EUV equipment since 2017, but more relevantly after 2022. Figure 2 illustrates the balance sheet indicators.

As for the other positive grade, ASML has a strong momentum situation. The firm is pushing towards the higher band of the one-year price range and above its 200 days moving average. In terms of valuation, its PE is aligned with my market average calculation, which considers a market-cap weighted average of forward PE. At last, the firm has a positive news sentiment in an AI classification method using recent news published about the company. These indicators are available on Figure 3. On Seeking Alpha, ASML momentum grade is B, with a strong price performance in comparison to all sector medians.

As for the Yield Grade, ASML is at the median of the researched companies. In Figure 4, ASML (the red dot) is compared to the other target companies and position itself with a 2.6% FCF Yield and a controlled debt environment. Initially, there is an indication of pricing power on the margins recorded. A comparison of ASML and Cadence (CDNS), which operate with completely different offers in the same semiconductor supply landscape, ASML depicts a premium Net Income. Moreover, the consistent performance is the result of a stable margin environment, as shown in Figure 5 and an improvement in the investment performance compared to the gross margin, as depicted in Figure 6. In this measure, which both Capex and R&D expenses are taken into consideration and compared to the Gross Margin generated, it is possible to evaluate the capacity of the firm to fund development efforts from its operation, without the need to further dilute capital or generate debt. In the last two years, it improved from 43% to 36% in this last quarter.

On a more negative note, current growth grade lags the median of researched firms. This is not an effect of ASML being stagnant but rather a relative measure, indicating that when compared to other target firms, mostly in the AI segment, there is a gap on the numbers of the firm, mostly when this comparison is performed against GPUs, AI applications, and infra construction. Figure 7 characterizes the revenues and gross margins of the firm in the last 20 quarters. Considering a flat gross margin scenario, ASML records revenue increase, but also a volatility resulting from natural cycles in the demand of foundries. In 2022, there was a supply chain disruption in the industry, that followed a demand exuberance that ended in a reduced customer appetite in 2024. This year’s story seems to be linked to export controls and a weak demand for part of the semiconductor DUV industry. In a comparison with Cadence, one can perceive that this behavior is not an idiosyncratic issue of ASML but rather a general market situation. In such case, investors may understand that an equity position in ASML will provide a different feature and risk factor exposure than other AI related stocks, such as Nvidia, for example. In the long run the expected growth in the industry should prevail and benefit ASML as well.

Conclusion

ASML published solid Q3 numbers, aligned with a growing market trend on the AI supply chain. The company’s strategic position is unique, as it enjoys a virtual monopoly in a critical component for the production of high-capacity chipsets. This situation embeds a consistent growth prospect, potentially volatile but long-term consistent, and the maintenance of pricing power. The firm’s cash generation and management is rational and cover both the investment and R&D needs and the shareholders current remuneration. Forward cash flow growth measures, arising from the future continuation of current results, may trigger strong price performance.

On the risk side, in the fundamental scoring system, a higher grade on momentum, compared to Yield and Growth may indicate that future results are priced closer to perfection than they could be, so investors keeping ASML in equity portfolios should evaluate the position under the scrutiny of market developments and potential short-term volatility in the stock is likely.

As a combination of both findings, ASML is a Hold and may be part of a suitability evaluation for a long position in equity portfolios, which will depend on each investor's personal situation.

data.title: Netflix Earnings Preview: Q3 2025
data.description:
Netflix Inc. (NFLX) will report third quarter 2025 results on Tuesday, Oct. 21, 2025. Here are the key numbers that we're watching.

The preview

Expectations have remained stable throughout the third quarter. In the previous three-month period, the company provided guidance for a stable outlook, in line with consensus. Visible Alpha consensus expects $11.5 billion in revenue for the third quarter and $45.1 billion for the full year 2025. Revenue is expected to be supported by continued new membership and monetization. Offering a range of pricing and plans, combined with continuing growth in the ads business, is expected to further increase monetization. Consensus expects the operating margin to increase to 31.5% in the third quarter and to 30.3% for full year 2025.

The company expects to grow revenue by increasing engagement trends and reducing churn while offering more diverse entertainment products. Gaming and the growth of ads could be key drivers in 2025. According to consensus, analysts now expect the company to generate a 30.3% margin, up from 29.6% in previous estimates, on expected revenue of $45.1 billion and $13.6 billion in operating profit in full year 2025.

In the spring, management highlighted that the Ad Tier enables lower prices. The company stated in the earnings call that they expect ad revenue to roughly double year over year again in 2025. Since early 2025, the ad-supported revenue estimate has been moving higher into the third quarter earnings release. While expectations are still below the initial forecast of $1.6 billion back in early 2024, the trend is improving and is now at $1.1 billion.

Netflix remains upbeat about the long-term opportunity, given the size of its user base. Co-CEO Gregory Peters explained that 2025 will be the year that the ads business will step up to the plate to swing. Currently, consensus projects total ad-supported revenue for full year 2027 to expand to $6.5 billion, but down from the expected $7.4 billion in 2024. There is a significant range of views on the magnitude of growth. For full year 2027, the analyst estimates range has widened from $3.5 billion to $9.2 billion to between $3.8 billion and $13.8 billion.

Based on Visible Alpha consensus, the operating profit margin is expected to grow from 26.7% in full year 2024 to 35.1% in full year 2027, an increase of 70 basis points since last quarter. Currently, consensus estimates the operating margin to exceed 32% by the end of 2026. There is significant debate among analysts with respect to full-year 2027 margin estimates, which range from 32% to 37%. This margin growth is expected to take full-year 2024 expected diluted EPS from $20.22 to $39.77, or 30x the full-year 2027 P/E, significantly higher than the 25x it traded in spring. The current consensus target price has ticked up to $1,400, or nearly 17% upside from the current levels.

Original Post
